# brain-for-web

> Interactive 3D brain atlas for the web, built with React and Three.js.

## What is this?

brain-for-web provides two npm packages for embedding an interactive 3D brain in web applications:

- **react-brain-atlas**: A React component library that renders a clickable, rotatable 3D brain using the Desikan–Killiany atlas (68 cortical regions mapped to 24 viewer regions). Built on React Three Fiber.
- **freesurfer-to-glb**: A CLI/Node.js tool that converts FreeSurfer atlas meshes into optimized GLB files for web use.

## Key facts

- Atlas: Desikan–Killiany (68 parcels → 24 interactive regions, 15 with full metadata)
- Output: Single GLB file, ~1–2 MB
- Rendering: WebGL via Three.js / React Three Fiber
- Features: Region selection, explode view, color modes (theme/neural system), camera fly-to, bilateral mirroring
- License: Apache 2.0 (code), CC BY-SA 3.0 (mesh data)
- Source meshes: Brain for Blender (Brainder.org) by Anderson M. Winkler

## Quickstart

```bash
npx freesurfer-to-glb --output public/models/brain-atlas.glb
npm install react-brain-atlas
```

```tsx
import { BrainAtlas } from 'react-brain-atlas';
<BrainAtlas glbPath="/models/brain-atlas.glb" />
```

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/mpatricny/brain-for-web
- npm (viewer): https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-brain-atlas
- npm (pipeline): https://www.npmjs.com/package/freesurfer-to-glb
